LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RECOLOGY, INC., RECOLOGY LEASING, INC.'S MOTION OF DEFENDANTS' FOR LEAVE TO AMEND THE ANSWER. THE MATTER IS CALLED. MS. KHUSHBOO PRESENTS AN EXPLANATION AND AN APOLOGY FOR THE LATE FILED OPPOSITION. MR. MIRSHAFIEI STATES HE WAS SERVED WITH THE OPPOSITION. THE COURT PROCEEDS, ARGUMENT IS HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS RECOLOGY, INC. AND RECOLOGY LEASING, INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND THE ANSWER IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants' For Leave To Amend The Answer
Off Calendar May-05-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Khushboo Vasandani, Esq. of B & D Law Group APLC, 310.424.5252, for Plaintiff;
Atty. Kaveh Mirshafiei, Esq. of Clapp Moroney Vucinich Beeman and Scheley, 650.989.5400, for Defendant.

DEFENDANT RECOLOGY, INC., RECOLOGY LEASING, INC.'s Motion Of Defendants' For Leave To Amend The Answer.

The matter is called. Ms. Khushboo presents an explanation and an apology for the late filed opposition. Ms. Khushboo notes that opposing counsel was served with the opposItion. Mr. Mirshafiei states the time he was served with the opposition noting that his reply addresses the arguments.

The Court proceeds, ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants Recology, Inc. and Recology Leasing, Inc.'s motion for leave to amend the answer is granted. Plaintiff did not file any opposition to the motion. In any event, the policy of liberality in allowing amendments supports the motion, Defendants have not unduly delayed or acted in bad faith in bringing the motion, and there is no showing that Plaintiff would be prejudiced.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email:RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
